title: Q3 2025 Open House: Opendoor 2.0 Charts Path to Profitability Through Software and AI
text: CEO Kaz Nejatian announced that Opendoor is refounding itself as a software and AI company during Q3 2025 earnings. The firm launched over a dozen AI-powered products and features, demonstrating significant innovation. Management stated they are driving to Adjusted Net Income breakeven by the end of 2026, measured on a twelve-month go-forward basis. Q3 2025 results showed the company achieving its profitability milestone while facing continued housing market headwinds. The strategic shift toward AI and software capabilities is reshaping Opendoor's risk-reward profile. Key operational improvements include AI-driven tools that have reduced buyer fall-through rates by double digits and lowered pre-list renovation costs by 10-20% in pilot markets. The company is also exploring new revenue streams through lead monetization and expanded marketplace capabilities. Margins have shown six consecutive months of improvement, and aged inventory has declined dramatically from 51% to much lower levels, indicating faster resale velocity and improved inventory health.
